title: Podman does not run WordPress container properly on Mac
body:
### Issue Description

Take the following compose.yaml file as an example. It runs without problem on Linux and Mac (using Podman Desktop):  

```
services:
  wp:
    image: ghcr.io/code-with-mehdi/wordpress:php8.2-apache
    env_file:
      - env.ini
    ports:
      - 8000:80
    volumes:
      - ./code:/var/www/html
    depends_on:
      - db
    
  db:
    image: ghcr.io/code-with-mehdi/mariadb:10.5
    env_file:
      - env.ini
    ports:
      - 6603:3306
    volumes:
      - dbvol:/var/lib/mysql
      - ./data:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d
  
  phpmyadmin:
    image: ghcr.io/code-with-mehdi/phpmyadmin:5.2-apache
    env_file:
      - env.ini
  


volumes:
  dbvol:

```
On Mac, though, the WordPress dashboard cannot add/remove plugins, showing the following dialog:  
![Screen Shot 2023-08-14 at 4 50 41 PM](https://github.com/containers/podman/assets/9320387/04b6506b-1e9e-42b1-a5d6-1c564e75d4c3)

This might seem like a WordPress issue, but it happens only on Mac when running containers with Podman, probably due to some mount limitations.


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Launch Podman Desktop
2. Run Docker Compose with given Compose file
3. Open WordPress dashboard and try to add/delete plugins



### Describe the results you received

The container seems to be readonly and cannot modify its content, though that's not the case on Linux.

### Describe the results you expected

Describe the results you expected
